question_title: Will the WTO post at least one new dispute-settlement news item naming India as complainant before May 1, 2026?
summary: The question asks whether the WTO will post at least one new dispute-settlement news item naming India as COMPLAINANT between March 10 and April 30, 2026. My research shows this is unlikely for several reasons: (1) India has not filed a new WTO complaint since 2018 (DS547 on US steel/aluminum, resolved in 2023), making the base rate ~1 per 2-3 years; (2) 18 days into the window, no qualifying items have appeared — all 2026 WTO news involving India shows India as RESPONDENT (China's DS642, DS644 complaints); (3) the February 2026 US-India trade deal (tariffs cut from 50% to 18%) signals de-escalation; (4) the WTO Appellate Body remains non-functional, discouraging new filings. The main upside risk is that India's 2025 retaliatory duty proposals on US steel/aluminum and auto tariffs could generate procedural news items, but these are suspension-of-concessions proceedings and may not appear as "complainant" items on the resolution source page. With 33 days remaining, a surprise filing is possible but unsupported by any current evidence.

factors.description: India's low base rate of filing new WTO complaints as complainant — approximately 1 new dispute every 2-3 years historically. The last new complaint was DS547 in May 2018, resolved in 2023. In a ~7 week window, the probability of a brand new filing is very low.

No evidence of any India-as-complainant dispute news item from March 10-28, 2026 (18 days into the resolution window). All 2026 WTO news items involving India show India as respondent (DS642, DS644 — both China complaints), not complainant.
US-India trade deal announced Feb 2, 2026 cut tariffs from 50% to 18%, signaling de-escalation of trade tensions and making new Indian complaints against the US less likely.

India proposed retaliatory duties via WTO on US steel/aluminum (May 2025) and auto tariffs (July 2025). If suspension-of-concessions proceedings advance during the window, they could generate a WTO news item naming India as complainant. However, these are distinct from new dispute filings.
factors.conditional: If India's suspension of concessions proposals advance to a WTO decision/arbitration step

factors.description: WTO Appellate Body remains non-functional since 2019, which generally discourages new dispute filings. India at MC14 is pushing for systemic reform, not filing new cases.
33 days remain in the resolution window (through April 30). Unforeseen events could trigger action, but no signals of imminent filings found.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 10% probability (YES)**

Key reasoning:
- India rarely files new WTO complaints (~1 every 2-3 years), and the last new one was 2018
- 18 days into the window, zero qualifying items have appeared; all 2026 India-WTO news involves India as respondent
- The Feb 2026 US-India trade deal signals de-escalation
- The WTO Appellate Body dysfunction discourages new filings
- Small upside from India's pending retaliatory duty proceedings and 33 remaining days in the window
sources_consulted: [WTO | dispute settlement - chronological list of disputes cases](https://www.wto.org/english/tratop_e/dispu_e/dispu_status_e.htm)
[2025 United States–India diplomatic and trade crisis - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_United_States%E2%80%93India_diplomatic_and_trade_crisis)

[India’s surprise tariff move on US goods suggests tougher stance on trade talks](https://www.scmp.com/week-asia/politics/article/3310343/does-indias-surprise-tariff-move-us-signal-tougher-stance-trade-or-just-symbolism)
[WTO | dispute settlement - DS547: United States � Certain Measures on Steel and Aluminium Products](https://www.wto.org/english/tratop_e/dispu_e/cases_e/ds547_e.htm)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the WTO posts at least one new dispute-settlement news item dated from March 10, 2026 through April 30, 2026 that names India as complainant. It resolves to no otherwise. The primary source is the WTO dispute-settlement news archive for India at https://www.wto.org/english/news_e/archive_e/country_subj_arc_e.htm?country1=IND&subject1=DISP
fine_print: Count only news items posted on or after March 10, 2026. The news item may concern a request for consultations, a panel request, an appeal-related step, or another formal dispute-settlement development, so long as India is the complainant in the official WTO item. Candidate Sources: WTO disputes page https://www.wto.org/english/tratop_e/dispu_e/dispu_status_e.htm
